sections.paragraph:
σιαίνομαι (Hippiatr. II 81, 5; POxy 1837, 2 [VI a.d.]; 1849, 2; Psaltes, Grammatik p. 333) be disturbed/annoyed (the act.=‘cause annoyance’ or ‘loathing’: schol. on Lucian p. 261, 22 Rabe) 1 Th 3:3 v.l.—S. vDobschütz ad loc. and the lit. s.v. σαίνω.—M-M.

Hippiatr. Hippiatr. = Corpus hippiatricorum Graecorum, IX a.d.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Psaltes, Grammatik Psaltes, Grammatik = SP., Grammatik der Byzantinischen Chroniken—List 6

act. act. = active

schol. schol. = scholion, scholia, scholiast

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 922
